seo_title: Memorial Necklace for Sister | Cremation Jewelry | Moon Raven Designs
meta_description: Handcrafted cremation pendants for a sister. Sterling silver and oxidized bronze. A private, grounded way to carry her presence.
intro:
The loss of a sister is a particular kind of absence. She was the one who knew the shape of your childhood, the keeper of shared secrets, the person you could be silent with. No piece of jewelry can fill that space, but a small, tangible object can sit against your skin and hold the weight of what you carry.

These are not sentimental trinkets. They are handcrafted vessels for ashes, fur, hair, or simply memory. Made by hand on Vancouver Island since 1974, each piece is meant to be worn quietly, touched without thinking, and kept close in a way that feels true to your sister and to you.
choosing: Choosing the right piece begins with how you want to wear her. A pendant rests near your heart, a ring is always in sight, a small urn pendant holds a pinch of ash or a lock of hair. Consider whether you want something visible or hidden, something that invites questions or remains private. Engraving can add a date, a word, a line from a song she loved. Size matters: a tiny piece can be more intimate, a larger one more present. You can also choose to carry nothing physical — just the memory, sealed in silver or bronze.
what_it_carries: What the piece holds is not just ash or hair. It holds the texture of her voice, the way she laughed, the argument you never resolved, the inside joke no one else gets. Wearing it is a private act — a daily touchstone for some, an occasional comfort for others. It does not promise to ease grief. It simply makes her absence tangible, something you can touch when words fail.
for_whom: These pieces are for sisters who have lost a sister — whether she was older, younger, estranged, or your closest confidant. They are for those who do not need to announce their loss but want a quiet way to keep her near. They are for the ones who find comfort in ritual, in the weight of metal, in the act of choosing a shape that feels like her.
blessing: May this small vessel hold what cannot be spoken. May it rest against your skin like a hand on your shoulder. May you find, in its weight, a thread of connection that grief cannot sever.

faq.q: What is a good sympathy gift for a sister?
faq.a: A memorial piece that can be worn daily is often the most meaningful. A cremation pendant or ring allows her to carry her sister with her in a private, personal way. Consider whether she might prefer something subtle or more visible, and whether she would want to include ashes or a lock of hair.

faq.q: What to say to someone who lost a sister?
faq.a: Simple, honest words are best. 'I am so sorry. I am here.' Avoid platitudes or trying to make it better. Acknowledge the uniqueness of the loss: 'I know she was your sister. That bond is irreplaceable.' Sometimes a quiet presence says more than any words.

A memorial necklace is a piece of jewelry designed to hold a small amount of cremated remains, hair, or another keepsake. It allows the wearer to keep a physical connection to the person they have lost. Our pendants are handcrafted in sterling silver or oxidized bronze and can be personalized with engraving.

faq.q: What do you get someone who is grieving a sister?
faq.a: A thoughtful, personal gift that acknowledges the depth of the loss. A memorial piece that can be worn or kept close, such as a cremation pendant or a ring. You might also consider a piece that reflects her sister's personality — a favorite symbol, stone, or engraving.

faq.q: How do you honor a sister who passed away?
faq.a: Honoring a sister can take many forms: wearing something that reminds you of her, visiting a place she loved, continuing a tradition you shared. A memorial pendant is one way to keep her physically close, a small ritual of remembrance that you can carry into your daily life.

A bereavement gift is given to someone who has experienced a loss. It should be sincere, respectful, and acknowledge the grief without trying to fix it. Memorial jewelry is a common choice because it offers a tangible way to hold the memory of the person who has died.
